header.title: Hash Generator — MD5, SHA-1, SHA-256 & SHA-3 Online
header.description: Free online hash generator supporting MD5, SHA-1, SHA-256, SHA-384, SHA-512, SHA3-256, and SHA3-512. Generate hashes from text or files with HMAC mode and hash verification. Compare hash algorithms and verify file integrity — 100% client-side and private.
header.keywords: hash generator, md5 hash generator, sha256 hash, sha1 hash, sha512 hash, sha3 hash generator, hmac calculator, hash verifier, file hash checker

privacy.notice: 100% client-side. Your data never leaves the browser. MD5 and SHA-3 use pure JS; SHA-1/2 use the Web Crypto API.

input.dropFile: Drop a file here or click to upload

meta.MD5: Fast but broken — never use for security

output.label: Hash Results
output.empty: Enter text or upload a file to generate hashes

howToUse.title: How to Use
howToUse.step1Desc: Type or paste text in the Text Input tab, or upload a file using the File Upload tab.
howToUse.step2Desc: Choose MD5, SHA-1, SHA-256, SHA-384, SHA-512, SHA3-256, or SHA3-512.
howToUse.step3Desc: Hashes are computed instantly. Optionally enable HMAC mode and enter a secret key.
howToUse.step4Desc: Paste an expected hash to verify, or copy the generated hash values to your clipboard.

items.question: Is MD5 still secure?
items.answer: No. MD5 is broken — it has known collision attacks. Use SHA-256 for security and SHA-3 for post-quantum readiness.

items.question: What is HMAC and when should I use it?
items.answer: HMAC is a keyed-hash message authentication code. Use it to verify both integrity and authenticity with a shared secret key.

items.question: Can hashes be reversed?
items.answer: No, hashes are one-way functions. However, unsalted hashes of common passwords can be looked up in rainbow tables.

items.question: What is the difference between SHA-2 and SHA-3?
items.answer: SHA-2 (SHA-256, SHA-512) uses a Merkle-Damgård structure. SHA-3 uses a sponge construction (Keccak) with a fundamentally different design.

items.question: Can I hash files with this tool?
items.answer: Yes. Switch to the File Upload tab and select any file. The hash is computed locally — your file never leaves your device.
